question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/transactionhandler/ConsensusSubmitMessageTransactionHandler.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: doUpdateTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a ConsensusSubmitMessage with a receipt where topicSequenceNumber is the same as an already-persisted message for the same topic, and since TopicMessage.isNew() always returns true (line 73 of TopicMessage.java) and the primary key is consensusTimestamp (not sequenceNumber), cause the mirror node to persist two messages with the same sequence number but different timestamps, corrupting the sequence number uniqueness invariant of the topic history?
